hero.title: Accessibility project hub
hero.description: Create projects, run scans, and keep your accessibility reviews organized in one polished workspace.

create.description: Set the name and root URL to start scanning.

messages.createError: Could not create project. Please try again.

hero.title: Project operations
hero.description: Run new accessibility scans and track their lifecycle from one place.

messages.runScanSuccess: Scan started. Waiting for backend processing...
messages.runScanError: Could not run scan. Please try again.
messages.emptyScans: No scans found for this project yet.

hero.title: Scan lifecycle and findings
hero.description: Track scan status, monitor summary metrics, and inspect accessibility issues in detail.

failed.title: Scan failed
failed.description: This scan ended in FAILED state. Review backend logs for execution details.

summary.title: Summary
summary.running: Summary is still being generated while the scan is running.
summary.unavailable: Summary is not available for this scan.

issues.title: Issues
issues.running: Scan is still running. Issues will appear as soon as they are available.
issues.empty: No issues were found for this scan.

issueGuide.show: What it means and how to fix it
issueGuide.readGuideLink: Open WCAG Guide to understand and prioritize this result

fallback.why: This rule detected a pattern that can reduce accessibility for some users.
fallback.impact: It may affect navigation, comprehension, or interaction for keyboard and screen-reader users.
fallback.step1: Inspect the element pointed by the selector and verify semantic HTML usage.
fallback.step2: Ensure accessible text, correct structure, and ARIA attributes only when needed.
fallback.step3: Run the scan again to confirm the issue is resolved.

messages.polling: Auto-refreshing this scan while backend status is RUNNING.
messages.loadError: Could not load scan details. Please try again.

hero.title: All scans across projects
hero.description: Track scan activity in one unified timeline and quickly open project or scan details.

messages.empty: No scans yet. Run your first scan from a project page.
messages.noMatches: No scans match the selected filters.
messages.loadError: Could not load scan history. Please try again.

hero.title: What WCAG is and how to use this app
hero.description: A quick guide to understand what Accessibility Copilot evaluates and how to turn findings into real improvements.

whatAppDoes.title: What the app does
whatAppDoes.description: It scans your pages and detects accessibility issues, showing severity, WCAG criterion, and actionable recommendations.

whatWcagIs.title: What WCAG is
whatWcagIs.description: WCAG is the international web accessibility standard. It defines practices so people with or without disabilities can use your site.

whyItMatters.title: Why it matters
whyItMatters.description: It improves real user experience, reduces friction in key tasks, and supports legal and digital quality expectations.

howToRead.title: How to read results
howToRead.item1: Severity indicates rough urgency (critical, serious, moderate, minor).
howToRead.item2: Rule and WCAG criterion explain which standard is not met.
howToRead.item3: Issue guidance explains user impact and concrete remediation steps.

flow.title: Recommended flow
flow.step3: Fix and validate on the real page.
flow.step4: Run the scan again to confirm improvements.

impactScale.title: Impact scale
impactScale.highDescription: May block tasks or comprehension for screen-reader and keyboard users.
impactScale.mediumDescription: Affects navigation or clarity in common scenarios, usually with partial workarounds.
impactScale.lowDescription: Usually not blocking, but fixing it improves consistency, quality, and overall UX.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.q1: Does this app replace manual accessibility audits?
faq.a1: No. It automates an important part, but manual review is still essential for real context and user experience.
faq.q2: Should I fix everything at once?
faq.a2: Not required. Start with high-impact issues, then medium, then low for a sustainable workflow.
faq.q3: Does every rule always apply the same way?
faq.a3: Not always. Use guidance as a baseline and validate each case in your actual UI and user flow.

hero.title: Page not found
hero.description: The page you requested does not exist or has been moved.

content.title: Let's get you back on track
content.description: Use one of these shortcuts to return to a valid workspace page.
